message.content:
You are a senior frontend engineer reviewing a pull request. Review the changes made to `.storybook/main.ts` at `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/.storybook/main.ts`.

Context: This is a React Native MFE that uses Storybook with `@storybook/react-native-web-vite`. The `@semparar/design-system-app` npm package is uncompiled (raw .ts/.tsx/.d.ts files). The changes fix two bugs that prevented Storybook from rendering DS components:

1. **dtsStubPlugin was defined but never registered** — The plugin intercepts `.d.ts` imports from `@semparar/*` packages and stubs them with runtime-safe exports. It was created but never added to `viteFinal` plugins array. Fix: registered it AND improved the `load()` to parse the actual `.d.ts` file and generate matching named exports (instead of just `export default {}`).

2. **Circular dependency in CardOptionDrag** — `CardOptionDrag/view.tsx` imports `CardOptionDragGroup` from `view.group.tsx`, and `view.group.tsx` imports `CardOptionDrag` from `view.tsx`. Vite's ESM evaluation hits a TDZ error. Fix: a new `circularFixPlugin` that stubs `./view.group` when imported from `CardOptionDrag/view.tsx`.

Please read the file and review:
- Correctness of the plugin implementations
- Edge cases that could break
- Any improvements needed
- Security concerns

Do NOT make changes, just review.

content.text: I'll review the `.storybook/main.ts` file to assess the plugin implementations and identify any issues.
Now let me look at the actual CardOptionDrag component to understand the circular dependency context better.

1→import React from 'react';
     2→import { Animated } from 'react-native';
     3→
     4→import { Box, TouchBox } from '../../atoms/Box';
     5→import Icon from '../../atoms/Icons';
     6→import { Text } from '../../atoms/Text';
     7→import normalize from '../../constants/normalize';
     8→import useCardOptionDrag from './index';
     9→import { PropsCardOptionDrag } from './Models';
    10→import CardOptionDragGroup from './view.group';
    11→
    12→function CardOptionDragComponent({
    13→  mb,
    14→  ml,
    15→  mr,
    16→  mt,
    17→  testID,
    18→  title,
    19→  icon,
    20→  iconColor = 'BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01',
    21→  iconActionLeft,

    32→  showIcon = false,
    33→  showIconRight = false,

    37→  onPressActionRight,
    38→  onPressActionIconLeft,
    39→  onPressActionIconRight,

    41→  disableOnPressActionIconRight = false,
    42→  onOpen,
    43→  isMoving,

    47→    panResponder,
    48→    CARD_SIZE,
    49→    hasBorderRadius,
    50→    truncatedTitle,
    51→    truncatedDescription,
    52→    allowedBgColor,
    53→    renderMarginLeft,
    54→  } = useCardOptionDrag({
    55→    animate,
    56→    closeCard,
    57→    bgColor,
    58→    title,
    59→    description,
    60→    onOpen,
    61→    isMoving,
    62→  });
    63→
    64→  return (
    65→    <Box mb={mb} ml={ml} mr={mr} mt={mt} testID={testID} width={width}>
    66→      <Box
    67→        width="100%"
    68→        height="100%"
    69→        direction
    70→        justify="space-between"
    71→        align="center"
    72→        position="absolute"
    73→        index={-1}
    74→        style={{ flex: 1 }}
    75→      >
    76→        <Box width="50%" height="100%" bgColor="EMPHASIS_COLOR_02">
    77→          <TouchBox
    78→            testID="DS-CardOptionDrag-onPressActionLeft"
    79→            width={CARD_SIZE}
    80→            height="100%"
    81→            justify="center"
    82→            align="center"
    83→            onPress={onPressActionLeft}
    84→          >
    85→            <Icon name={iconActionLeft} size="xs" color="INTENSE_COLOR_01" />
    86→            <Text
    87→              mt="nano"
    88→              type="paragraphMD"
    89→              color="INTENSE_COLOR_01"
    90→              maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.4}
    91→            >
    92→              {textActionLeft}
    93→            </Text>
    94→          </TouchBox>
    95→        </Box>
    96→
    97→        <Box
    98→          width="50%"
    99→          height="100%"
   100→          align="flex-end"
   101→          bgColor="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01"
   102→        >
   103→          <TouchBox
   104→            testID="DS-CardOptionDrag-onPressActionRight"
   105→            width={CARD_SIZE}
   106→            height="100%"
   107→            justify="center"
   108→            align="center"
   109→            onPress={onPressActionRight}
   110→          >
   111→            <Icon name={iconActionRight} size="xs" color="INTENSE_COLOR_01" />
   112→            <Text
   113→              mt="nano"
   114→              type="paragraphMD"
   115→              color="INTENSE_COLOR_01"
   116→              maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.4}
   117→            >
   118→              {textActionRight}
   119→            </Text>
   120→          </TouchBox>
   121→        </Box>
   122→      </Box>
   123→      <Animated.View
   124→        testID="DS-CardOptionDrag-AnimatedView"
   125→        style={[{ transform: [{ translateX: pan.x }] }, { width: '100%' }]}
   126→        {...panResponder.panHandlers}
   127→      >
   128→        <TouchBox
   129→          testID="DS-CardOptionDrag-onPress"
   130→          disabled={disabled}
   131→          activeOpacity={1}
   132→          onPress={onPress}
   133→          width="100%"
   134→          direction
   135→          bgColor={allowedBgColor}
   136→          br={hasBorderRadius ? 'half' : 'none'}
   137→          pd="stretchGSized"
   138→          justify="space-between"
   139→          align="flex-start"
   140→        >
   141→          {isHighlighted && (

   146→              height={normalize(24)}

   154→                br="half"
   155→                mb="nano"
   156→              />
   157→            </Box>
   158→          )}
   159→
   160→          {showIcon && !showIconRight && (
   161→            <TouchBox
   162→              onPress={onPressActionIconLeft}
   163→              disabled={disabledOnPressActionIconLeft}
   164→              testID="DS-CardOptionDrag-IconLeft"
   165→            >
   166→              <Icon

   168→                mr="smaller"
   169→                name={icon}
   170→                color={iconColor}
   171→                size="xs"
   172→              />
   173→            </TouchBox>
   174→          )}
   175→
   176→          <Box
   177→            ml={renderMarginLeft(showIcon, showIconRight, isHighlighted)}
   178→            width="100%"
   179→            flex={1}
   180→          >
   181→            <Text
   182→              type="paragraphXL"
   183→              color="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01"
   184→              maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.2}
   185→            >
   186→              {truncatedTitle}
   187→            </Text>
   188→
   189→            <Text
   190→              mt="nano"
   191→              type="paragraphMD"
   192→              color="INTENSE_COLOR_04"
   193→              maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.2}
   194→            >
   195→              {truncatedDescription}
   196→            </Text>
   197→
   198→            <Text
   199→              mt="xxxxs"
   200→              type="paragraphSM"
   201→              color="INTENSE_COLOR_03"
   202→              maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.2}
   203→            >
   204→              {textFooter}
   205→            </Text>
   206→          </Box>
   207→
   208→          {showIcon && showIconRight && (
   209→            <TouchBox
   210→              onPress={onPressActionIconRight}
   211→              disabled={disableOnPressActionIconRight}
   212→              testID="DS-CardOptionDrag-IconRight"
   213→            >
   214→              <Icon
   215→                testID="DS-CardOptionDrag-Icon"
   216→                ml="smaller"
   217→                name={icon}
   218→                color={iconColor}
   219→                size="xs"
   220→              />
   221→            </TouchBox>
   222→          )}
   223→        </TouchBox>
   224→      </Animated.View>
   225→    </Box>
   226→  );
   227→}
   228→
   229→CardOptionDragComponent.displayName = 'CardOptionDragComponent';
   230→export const CardOptionDrag = Object.assign(CardOptionDragComponent, {
   231→  Group: CardOptionDragGroup,
   232→});
   233→

content.text: Now let me research Vite plugin best practices and potential security concerns.

input.query: Vite plugin security concerns file system access resolveId load 2026
Vite plugin circular dependency handling ESM TDZ error best practices

content.content:
Web search results for query: "Vite plugin security concerns file system access resolveId load 2026"

Links: [{"title":"Unrestricted file system access warning · Issue #179 · vite-pwa/vite-plugin-pwa","url":"https://github.com/vite-pwa/vite-plugin-pwa/issues/179"},{"title":"False negative: Unrestricted file system access to \"<PROJECT_ROOT>/src/main.tsx\" on HMR · Issue #4331 · vitejs/vite","url":"https://github.com/vitejs/vite/issues/4331"},{"title":"Fix \"Unrestricted file system access to\" messages by paescuj · Pull Request #8486 · directus/directus","url":"https://github.com/directus/directus/pull/8486"},{"title":"vite vulnerabilities | Snyk","url":"https://security.snyk.io/package/npm/vite"},{"title":"virtual file system is broken in v6 · Issue #18223 · vitejs/vite","url":"https://github.com/vitejs/vite/issues/18223"},{"title":"Vite does not run the resolveId hook for plugins · Issue #13111 · vitejs/vite","url":"https://github.com/vitejs/vite/issues/13111"},{"title":"[plugin commonjs--resolver] It appears a plugin has implemented a \"resolveId\" hook that uses \"this.resolve\" without forwarding the third \"options\" parameter of \"resolveId\". · Issue #14217 · sveltejs/kit","url":"https://github.com/sveltejs/kit/issues/14217"},{"title":"Vite Error: Unrestricted file system access ... · Issue #28 · jpkleemans/vite-svg-loader","url":"https://github.com/jpkleemans/vite-svg-loader/issues/28"},{"title":"Troubleshooting | Vite","url":"https://vite.dev/guide/troubleshooting"},{"title":"CVE-2025-30208: Vite File Disclosure Vulnerability","url":"https://www.sentinelone.com/vulnerability-database/cve-2025-30208/"}]

Based on the search results, here are the key findings related to Vite plugin security concerns around file system access, `resolveId`, and `load` hooks:

## Unrestricted File System Access Warnings

Vite issues warnings about unrestricted filesystem access when plugins access files, with messages indicating that "for security concerns, accessing files outside of serving allow list will be restricted." This affects plugins that trigger unrestricted file system access to files, with Vite warning that these restrictions will be enforced by default in future versions.

## resolveId and load Hook Issues

When Rollup plugins are used in Vite, the resolveId hook may not run, causing the plugin to break. Additionally, improper implementation of the resolveId hook (such as using "this.resolve" without forwarding the third "options" parameter) can lead to wrong module resolutions and cause issues with the node-resolve and commonjs plugins.

## Recent Vulnerability

CVE-2025-30208 is an information disclosure vulnerability in Vite that allows attackers to bypass file access restrictions and read arbitrary files.

## Mitigation

Developers can configure the server.fs.allow option to allow Vite to access files and folders outside of the project root.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Web search results for query: "Vite plugin circular dependency handling ESM TDZ error best practices"

Links: [{"title":"Troubleshooting | Vite","url":"https://vite.dev/guide/troubleshooting"},{"title":"vite-plugin-circular-dependency - npm","url":"https://www.npmjs.com/package/vite-plugin-circular-dependency"},{"title":"Troubleshooting | Vite (main branch)","url":"https://main.vitejs.dev/guide/troubleshooting"},{"title":"Troubleshooting | Vite","url":"https://v4.vitejs.dev/guide/troubleshooting"},{"title":"How do you guys handle circular imports? · vitejs/vite · Discussion #16424","url":"https://github.com/vitejs/vite/discussions/16424"},{"title":"Error message for circular dependencies if vite built while using vite v4.4.9, vue3.3.4 · vitejs/vite · Discussion #14090","url":"https://github.com/vitejs/vite/discussions/14090"},{"title":"warn if HMR failed due a circular dependency issue · Issue #7893 · vitejs/vite","url":"https://github.com/vitejs/vite/issues/7893"},{"title":"How to avoid circular dependency in your Vite applications for proper HMR — Provash's Blog","url":"https://provash.dev/blog/avoid-circular-dependency-in-vite/"},{"title":"SSR support circular dependency · Issue #2258 · vitejs/vite","url":"https://github.com/vitejs/vite/issues/2258"},{"title":"Bundling a Vite app is creating unexpected circular dependencies · Issue #20202 · vitejs/vite","url":"https://github.com/vitejs/vite/issues/20202"}]

Based on the search results, here are the key findings about Vite plugin circular dependency handling, ESM TDZ errors, and best practices:

## Circular Dependency Handling in Vite

If HMR is handled but it is within a circular dependency, a full reload will also happen to recover the execution order. To solve this, try breaking the loop.

### Debugging Circular Dependencies

You can run `vite --debug hmr` to log the circular dependency path if a file change triggered it.

### Plugin-Based Solutions

For those using Vite 7.0.2 or newer, there's a plugin approach available. A plugin can use the new `hotUpdate()` hook (with `handleHotUpdate()` being deprecated) to clear circular dependency references by setting `importedModules` and `importers` to empty Sets on modules.

## ESM and TDZ Context

The search results reference the broader context that Vite cannot handle and does not support code that only runs on non-strict mode (sloppy mode), because Vite uses ESM and it is always strict mode inside ESM.

## General Best Practice

Scalable ways to remove circular dependencies can help avoid unexpected HMR behaviors in Vite projects.

The fundamental recommendation is to refactor your code structure to break circular dependency loops rather than relying on workarounds, though the plugin-based HMR clearing approach can serve as a temporary solution when refactoring isn't immediately feasible.
